sentences.sentence: Over the past century, a powerful idea has taken root in the educational landscape.
sentences.translation: 在过去一个世纪里，一个有影响力的观念已经在教育领域扎下了根。
difficult_points: Over the past century 表示从过去延续到现在的时间范围，因此主句使用现在完成时。
take root 是比喻表达，表示一个观念逐渐被接受并稳定存在。
educational landscape 指教育领域的整体环境，而不是具体的自然景观。

sentences.sentence: The concept of intelligence as something innate has been supplanted by the idea that intelligence is not fixed, and that, with the right training, we can be the authors of our own cognitive capabilities.
sentences.translation: 把智力视为某种天生特质的观念，已经被另一种观念取代：智力并非固定不变，而且通过适当训练，我们可以成为自身认知能力的塑造者。

structure.text: The concept of intelligence as something innate
and that, with the right training, we can be the authors of our own cognitive capabilities

difficult_points: 主语较长，核心是 The concept，of intelligence as something innate 修饰这个观念的内容。
has been supplanted 是现在完成时被动语态，强调旧观念已被新观念取代。
两个 that 从句并列说明 the idea 的具体内容。
be the authors of our own cognitive capabilities 是比喻说法，意思是可以主动塑造自己的认知能力。

sentences.sentence: Psychologist Alfred Binet, the developer of the first intelligence tests, was one of many 19th-century scientists who held that earlier view and sought to quantify cognitive ability.
sentences.translation: 心理学家阿尔弗雷德·比奈是第一批智力测试的开发者，也是许多持有这种早期观点并试图量化认知能力的 19 世纪科学家之一。

structure.text: the developer of the first intelligence tests
who held that earlier view and sought to quantify cognitive ability

difficult_points: the developer of the first intelligence tests 是插入同位语，补充说明 Alfred Binet 的身份。
who 引导定语从句，修饰 scientists，而不是只修饰 Alfred Binet。
held 和 sought 是定语从句中的并列谓语，共同说明这些科学家的观点和行为。

sentences.sentence: Then, in the early 20th century, progressive thinkers revolted against the notion that inherent ability is destiny.
sentences.translation: 后来，在 20 世纪初，进步思想家开始反对“天生能力决定命运”这一观念。
difficult_points: Then 和 in the early 20th century 共同交代历史阶段的转折。
revolt against 在这里不是武装反叛，而是思想上强烈反对。
that 从句解释 the notion 的内容，即“天赋能力就是命运”。

sentences.sentence: Instead, educators such as John Dewey argued that every child's intelligence could be developed, given the right environment.
sentences.translation: 相反，像约翰·杜威这样的教育家认为，只要有合适的环境，每个孩子的智力都可以得到发展。

structure.text: that every child's intelligence could be developed

difficult_points: Instead 标示与前一句“天赋决定命运”的观念形成对照。
that 引导宾语从句，说明教育家主张的具体内容。
given the right environment 是条件含义，意为“如果有合适的环境”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在有合适环境的情况下

sentences.sentence: 'Growth mindset theory' is a relatively new – and extremely popular – version of this idea.
sentences.translation: “成长型思维理论”是这一观念的一个相对较新且极受欢迎的版本。
difficult_points: 破折号插入 and extremely popular，用来补充强调这一理论的流行程度。
this idea 回指上一段中“智力可以被发展”的观念。
version of this idea 表明成长型思维并非全新思想，而是该思想的新形式。

sentences.sentence: In many schools today you will see hallways covered in motivational posters and hear speeches on the mindset of great sporting heroes who simply believed their way to the top.
sentences.translation: 在如今许多学校里，你会看到走廊贴满励志海报，也会听到关于伟大体育英雄思维方式的演讲，说他们只是凭着信念一路走向顶峰。

structure.text: will see hallways covered in motivational posters
and hear speeches on the mindset of great sporting heroes
who simply believed their way to the top

difficult_points: see 和 hear 是并列谓语，共用主语 you。
covered in motivational posters 是过去分词短语，修饰 hallways。
who 引导定语从句，修饰 great sporting heroes。
believed their way to the top 是夸张表达，指似乎仅靠信念取得顶尖成就。

sentences.sentence: A major focus of the growth mindset in schools is coaxing students away from seeing failure as an indication of their ability, and towards seeing it as a chance to improve that ability.
sentences.translation: 学校中成长型思维的一个主要重点，是引导学生不要把失败看作能力的证明，而要把它看作提升这种能力的机会。

structure.text: A major focus of the growth mindset in schools
away from seeing failure as an indication of their ability
and towards seeing it as a chance to improve that ability

difficult_points: 主语核心是 A major focus，of the growth mindset in schools 是后置修饰。
coaxing students away from ... and towards ... 表示把学生从一种理解方式引向另一种理解方式。
seeing failure as 和 seeing it as 形成平行结构，it 回指 failure。
to improve that ability 修饰 chance，说明机会的用途。

sentences.sentence: As educationalist Jeff Howard noted several decades ago: 'Smart is not something that you just are, smart is something that you can get.'
sentences.translation: 正如教育家杰夫·霍华德几十年前所指出的：“聪明不是你天生就是的东西，聪明是你可以获得的东西。”

structure.text: As educationalist Jeff Howard noted several decades ago

difficult_points: As 引导方式状语从句，引出后面的引用。
两个 something 后各有 that 定语从句，分别说明“不是本来就是”和“可以获得”。
Smart 在引语中被名词化使用，指“聪明这件事”。

sentences.sentence: The idea of the growth mindset is based on the work of psychologist Carol Dweck in California in the 1990s.
sentences.translation: 成长型思维这一观念以心理学家卡罗尔·德韦克 20 世纪 90 年代在加利福尼亚的研究为基础。

structure.text: The idea of the growth mindset

difficult_points: The idea of the growth mindset 的核心是 idea，of 短语说明是哪种观念。
is based on 表示理论基础，而不是物理位置。
in California in the 1990s 连续两个介词短语分别交代地点和时间。

sentences.sentence: In one key experiment, Dweck divided a group of 10- to 12-year-olds into two groups.
sentences.translation: 在一项关键实验中，德韦克把一组 10 到 12 岁的孩子分成了两组。

structure.text: divided a group of 10- to 12-year-olds into two groups

difficult_points: In one key experiment 放在句首，限定后面行为发生的研究背景。
10- to 12-year-olds 是名词化表达，指 10 到 12 岁的孩子。
divide ... into ... 表示把一个整体分成若干部分。

sentences.sentence: All were told that they had achieved a high score on a test but the first group were praised for their intelligence in achieving this, while the others were praised for their effort.
sentences.translation: 所有孩子都被告知他们在一次测试中取得了高分，但第一组因为取得这一成绩所体现出的聪明而受到表扬，而另一组则因为努力而受到表扬。

structure.text: that they had achieved a high score on a test
but the first group were praised for their intelligence in achieving this
while the others were praised for their effort

difficult_points: All 指前一句中所有参与实验的孩子，后面使用被动语态 were told。
that 从句说明他们被告知的内容，其中 had achieved 表示在被告知之前已经取得高分。
but 连接对比内容，说明两组受到表扬的原因不同。
while 引导对照关系，表示另一组因努力而受到表扬。

sentences.sentence: The second group – those who had been instilled with a 'growth mindset' – were subsequently far more likely to put effort into future tasks.
sentences.translation: 第二组——也就是那些被灌输了“成长型思维”的孩子——后来更有可能在未来任务中投入努力。

structure.text: those who had been instilled with a 'growth mindset'
who had been instilled with a 'growth mindset'
to put effort into future tasks

difficult_points: 破折号中的 those who ... 是同位语，解释 The second group 指哪一组。
had been instilled 是过去完成时被动语态，表示在后续行为之前已经被灌输某种观念。
far more likely to 强调可能性明显更高。
put effort into 表示向某事投入努力，而不是单纯“放置努力”。

sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, the former took on only those tasks that would not risk their sense of worth.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，前一组只接受那些不会危及他们自我价值感的任务。

structure.text: that would not risk their sense of worth

difficult_points: the former 指前面提到的第一组，即因聪明而受到表扬的那组。
only 限定 those tasks，强调他们选择任务的范围很窄。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 tasks。
risk their sense of worth 表示威胁他们的自我价值感。

sentences.sentence: This group had inferred that success or failure is due to innate ability, and this 'fixed mindset' had led them to fear of failure and lack of effort.
sentences.translation: 这一组推断成功或失败取决于天生能力，而这种“固定型思维”使他们害怕失败并缺乏努力。

structure.text: that success or failure is due to innate ability
and this 'fixed mindset' had led them
to fear of failure and lack of effort

difficult_points: had inferred 使用过去完成时，表示这种推断发生在后续表现之前。
that 从句是 inferred 的宾语，说明他们形成的理解。
this 'fixed mindset' 回指前一分句中把成败归因于天生能力的观念。
led them to 后接名词短语，表示造成“害怕失败”和“缺乏努力”的结果。

sentences.sentence: Praising ability actually made the students perform worse, while praising effort emphasised that change was possible.
sentences.translation: 表扬能力实际上让学生表现得更差，而表扬努力则强调了改变是可能的。

structure.text: actually made the students perform worse

difficult_points: Praising ability 是动名词短语作主语，表示“表扬能力”这一行为。
made the students perform worse 是 make + 宾语 + 动词原形结构。
while 引导对照关系，把表扬能力和表扬努力的影响进行比较。
that change was possible 是 emphasised 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: One of the greatest impediments to successfully implementing a growth mindset, however, is the education system itself: in many parts of the world, the school climate is obsessed with performance in the form of constant testing, analysing and ranking of students – a key characteristic of the fixed mindset.
sentences.translation: 然而，成功实施成长型思维的最大障碍之一，正是教育系统本身：在世界许多地方，学校氛围沉迷于以持续测试、分析和学生排名为形式的表现评估——这正是固定型思维的一个关键特征。

structure.text: One of the greatest impediments to successfully implementing a growth mindset
in many parts of the world
the school climate is obsessed with performance
in the form of constant testing, analysing and ranking of students
a key characteristic of the fixed mindset

difficult_points: 主语核心是 One，of the greatest impediments 和 to successfully implementing a growth mindset 层层修饰。
however 插入在主谓之间，表示与前面实验支持形成转折。
冒号后面的分句解释为什么教育系统本身是障碍。
破折号后的 a key characteristic of the fixed mindset 对前面的做法作总结评价。

sentences.sentence: Nor is it unusual for schools to create a certain cognitive dissonance, when they applaud the benefits of a growth mindset but then hand out fixed target grades in lessons based on performance.
sentences.translation: 学校制造出某种认知失调也并不罕见：它们一边称赞成长型思维的益处，一边又在基于表现的课堂中发放固定目标成绩。

structure.text: for schools to create a certain cognitive dissonance
when they applaud the benefits of a growth mindset
but then hand out fixed target grades

difficult_points: Nor 置于句首引起部分倒装，is 提到形式主语 it 前面。
it 是形式主语，真正说明内容的是 for schools to create a certain cognitive dissonance。
when 引导的从句说明认知失调发生的具体情境。
applaud 和 hand out 是并列动作，形成学校口头支持与实际做法之间的矛盾。

sentences.sentence: Aside from the implementation problem, the original growth mindset research has also received harsh criticism.
sentences.translation: 除了实施方面的问题外，最初的成长型思维研究也受到了严厉批评。
difficult_points: Aside from 引出除前一段实施问题之外的另一类问题。
has received 使用现在完成时，表示批评持续影响到现在。
harsh criticism 表示强烈而严厉的批评。

sentences.sentence: The statistician Andrew Gelman claims that 'their research designs have enough degrees of freedom that they could take their data to support just about any theory at all'.
sentences.translation: 统计学家安德鲁·盖尔曼声称，“他们的研究设计有足够多的自由度，以至于他们几乎可以让数据支持任何理论”。

structure.text: that 'their research designs have enough degrees of freedom
that they could take their data to support just about any theory at all

difficult_points: claims 后接 that 宾语从句，宾语从句内容以直接引语形式呈现。
enough ... that ... 构成结果结构，表示自由度多到足以导致后面的结果。
take their data to support 指用同一批数据解释成支持某种理论。
just about any theory at all 强调“几乎任何理论”，语气带有批判性。

sentences.sentence: Professor of Psychology Timothy Bates, who has been trying to replicate Dweck's work, is finding that the results are repeatedly null.
sentences.translation: 心理学教授蒂莫西·贝茨一直试图复现德韦克的研究，他发现结果反复为空，即没有显著效果。

structure.text: who has been trying to replicate Dweck's work
that the results are repeatedly null

difficult_points: who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充 Bates 正在做的研究工作。
has been trying 是现在完成进行时，强调复现实验的尝试持续进行。
is finding 使用现在进行时，表现研究发现仍在形成或持续出现。
null 在研究语境中指结果不显著或没有发现预期效果。

sentences.sentence: He notes that: 'People with a growth mindset don't cope any better with failure … Kids with the growth mindset aren't getting better grades, either before or after our intervention study.'
sentences.translation: 他指出：“具有成长型思维的人并没有更好地应对失败……具有成长型思维的孩子也没有取得更好的成绩，无论是在我们的干预研究之前还是之后。”

structure.text: that: 'People with a growth mindset don't cope any better with failure
Kids with the growth mindset aren't getting better grades
either before or after our intervention study

difficult_points: He 指前一句的 Timothy Bates。
notes that 后面用冒号引出直接引用，引用内部包含两个相关判断。
don't cope any better with failure 中 any better 用于否定句，表示“并没有更好”。
either before or after 表示干预研究前后都没有更好成绩。

sentences.sentence: Much of this criticism is not lost on Dweck, and she deserves great credit for responding to it and adapting her work accordingly.
sentences.translation: 德韦克并非没有注意到这些批评中的许多内容，而且她因回应这些批评并相应调整自己的研究而值得高度肯定。

structure.text: for responding to it and adapting her work accordingly

difficult_points: is not lost on Dweck 是习语，表示德韦克意识到了这些批评。
and 连接两个并列分句，先说明她注意到批评，再评价她的回应。
responding to it 和 adapting her work accordingly 是并列动名词短语，说明她值得肯定的原因。
accordingly 表示根据批评作出相应调整。

sentences.sentence: In fact, she argues that her work has been misunderstood and misapplied in a range of ways.
sentences.translation: 事实上，她认为自己的研究在很多方面被误解并被误用。

structure.text: that her work has been misunderstood and misapplied

difficult_points: In fact 用来进一步解释德韦克对批评的回应。
that 引导 argues 的宾语从句。
has been misunderstood and misapplied 是现在完成时被动语态，说明她的研究一直存在被误解和误用的问题。
in a range of ways 表示误解和误用有多种形式。

sentences.sentence: She has also expressed concerns that her theories are being misappropriated in schools by being conflated with the self-esteem movement: 'For me the growth mindset is a tool for learning and improvement. It's not just a vehicle for making children feel good.'
sentences.translation: 她还表达了担忧：她的理论在学校中被与自尊运动混为一谈，从而遭到误用。她说：“对我来说，成长型思维是学习和进步的工具。它不只是让孩子感觉良好的载体。”

structure.text: that her theories are being misappropriated in schools
by being conflated with the self-esteem movement
'For me the growth mindset is a tool for learning and improvement
It's not just a vehicle for making children feel good

difficult_points: concerns 后的 that 从句说明她担忧的具体内容。
are being misappropriated 是现在进行时被动语态，强调她认为误用正在发生。
by being conflated with 表示误用的方式，即被与自尊运动混同。
冒号后的两句直接引语解释她眼中成长型思维的真正用途。

expressions.text: a tool for learning and improvement

sentences.sentence: But there is another factor at work here.
sentences.translation: 但这里还有另一个因素在起作用。
difficult_points: But 表示从研究误用转向另一个解释因素。
there is 是存在句，真正的新信息是 another factor。
at work here 是习语，表示某因素正在此处发挥作用。

sentences.sentence: The failure to translate the growth mindset into the classroom might reflect a misunderstanding of the nature of teaching and learning itself.
sentences.translation: 未能把成长型思维转化到课堂实践中，可能反映了对教学与学习本质本身的误解。

structure.text: The failure to translate the growth mindset into the classroom
a misunderstanding of the nature of teaching and learning itself
to translate the growth mindset into the classroom

difficult_points: 主语核心是 The failure，后面的 to translate ... 说明失败的具体内容。
translate ... into the classroom 是比喻用法，指把理念转化为课堂实践。
might reflect 表示作者提出一种可能解释，语气并非绝对。
itself 强调误解的是教学和学习的本质。

sentences.sentence: Growth mindset supporters David Yeager and Gregory Walton claim that interventions should be delivered in a subtle way to maximise their effectiveness.
sentences.translation: 成长型思维的支持者大卫·耶格和格雷戈里·沃尔顿声称，干预应以微妙的方式实施，以最大化其效果。

structure.text: Growth mindset supporters David Yeager and Gregory Walton
that interventions should be delivered in a subtle way

difficult_points: David Yeager and Gregory Walton 与 Growth mindset supporters 构成同位关系，说明支持者是谁。
that 从句是 claim 的宾语，表达他们的主张。
should be delivered 是情态动词加被动语态，说明干预应如何实施。
to maximise their effectiveness 是目的状语，说明微妙实施的目的。

expressions.text: be delivered in a subtle way

sentences.sentence: They say that if adolescents perceive a teacher's intervention as conveying that they are in need of help, this could undo its intended effects.
sentences.translation: 他们说，如果青少年把教师的干预理解为在传达他们需要帮助的信息，这可能会抵消干预原本想达到的效果。

structure.text: that if adolescents perceive a teacher's intervention as conveying that they are in need of help
if adolescents perceive a teacher's intervention as conveying
that they are in need of help
this could undo its intended effects

difficult_points: They 指前一句的 Yeager 和 Walton。
say 后的 that 宾语从句内部又包含 if 条件从句。
perceive ... as conveying 表示“把……理解为在传达……”。
this 回指前面整个条件情境，即学生认为老师在暗示他们需要帮助。

sentences.sentence: A lot of what drives students is their innate beliefs and how they perceive themselves.
sentences.translation: 驱动学生的很大一部分因素，是他们内在的信念以及他们如何看待自己。

structure.text: A lot of what drives students

difficult_points: what drives students 是名词性从句，作介词 of 的宾语。
A lot of what drives students 整体作主语，表示驱动学生的许多因素。
their innate beliefs 和 how they perceive themselves 是并列表语。
perceive themselves 指学生对自我的看法。

sentences.sentence: There is a strong correlation between self-perception and achievement, but there is evidence to suggest that the actual effect of achievement on self-perception is stronger than the other way round.
sentences.translation: 自我认知与成就之间存在很强的相关性，但有证据表明，成就对自我认知的实际影响比反过来的影响更强。

structure.text: There is a strong correlation between self-perception and achievement
to suggest that the actual effect of achievement on self-perception is stronger
that the actual effect of achievement on self-perception is stronger than the other way round

difficult_points: 第一个 There is 引出相关性，but 后第二个 there is 转入证据。
to suggest 是不定式短语，修饰 evidence，说明证据指向什么结论。
that 从句是 suggest 的宾语从句。
the other way round 指自我认知对成就的影响方向。

sentences.sentence: To stand up in a classroom and successfully deliver a good speech is a genuine achievement, and that is likely to be more powerfully motivating than vague notions of 'motivation' itself.
sentences.translation: 站在教室里并成功发表一场出色演讲，本身就是一项真正的成就，而这很可能比“动机”本身这种模糊概念更能激励人。

structure.text: To stand up in a classroom and successfully deliver a good speech
and that is likely to be more powerfully motivating

difficult_points: To stand up ... and successfully deliver ... 是不定式短语作主语。
that 回指前面“成功演讲是一项真正成就”这件事。
is likely to be 表示可能性较高，但不是绝对断言。
than 引出比较对象，说明实际成就比抽象的动机概念更有激励作用。

sentences.sentence: Recent evidence would suggest that growth mindset interventions are not the elixir of student learning that its proponents claim it to be.
sentences.translation: 近期证据似乎表明，成长型思维干预并不是其支持者所声称的学生学习灵丹妙药。

structure.text: that growth mindset interventions are not the elixir of student learning
that its proponents claim it to be

difficult_points: would suggest 语气较委婉，表示证据倾向于支持某种结论。
that 引导宾语从句，说明近期证据暗示的内容。
the elixir of student learning 是比喻，指能解决学生学习问题的灵丹妙药。
that its proponents claim it to be 修饰 elixir，说明这是支持者声称的效果。

sentences.sentence: The growth mindset appears to be a viable construct in the lab, which, when administered in the classroom via targeted interventions, doesn't seem to work.
sentences.translation: 成长型思维在实验室中似乎是一个可行的理论构念，但当它通过有针对性的干预被应用于课堂时，似乎并不起作用。

structure.text: appears to be a viable construct in the lab
when administered in the classroom via targeted interventions

difficult_points: appears to be 表示“似乎是”，语气保留余地。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，回指 The growth mindset 或这一构念。
when administered ... 是省略结构，相当于 when it is administered。
via targeted interventions 说明进入课堂的方式是通过有针对性的干预。

sentences.sentence: It is hard to dispute that having faith in the capacity to change is a good attribute for students.
sentences.translation: 很难否认，相信自己有改变的能力对学生来说是一种良好品质。

structure.text: that having faith in the capacity to change is a good attribute for students
having faith in the capacity to change

difficult_points: It is hard to dispute 是形式主语结构，真正被评价的内容在 that 从句中。
that 从句中 having faith ... 是动名词短语作主语。
the capacity to change 表示“改变的能力”，to change 修饰 capacity。
attribute 在这里指品质或特质。

sentences.sentence: Paradoxically, however, that aspiration is not well served by direct interventions that try to instil it.
sentences.translation: 然而，矛盾的是，试图灌输这种信念的直接干预并不能很好地服务于这一愿望。
difficult_points: Paradoxically 和 however 共同提示作者要提出一个看似矛盾的转折。
that aspiration 回指前一句中学生应相信改变能力这一愿望。
is not well served 是被动语态，表示这种愿望并未因某种做法得到很好实现。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 direct interventions。

sentences.sentence: Motivational posters and talks are often a waste of time, and might well give students a deluded notion of what success actually means.
sentences.translation: 励志海报和演讲往往是在浪费时间，而且很可能会让学生对成功真正意味着什么产生一种误导性的认识。

structure.text: are often a waste of time
and might well give students a deluded notion

difficult_points: Motivational posters and talks 是并列主语。
are 和 might well give 是并列谓语，分别说明浪费时间和可能带来的错误影响。
might well 表示“很可能”，语气比普通 might 更强。
what success actually means 是名词性从句，作 of 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Teaching concrete skills such as how to write an effective introduction to an essay then praising students' effort in getting there is probably a far better way of improving confidence than telling them how unique they are, or indeed how capable they are of changing their own brains.
sentences.translation: 教授具体技能，例如如何为一篇文章写出有效的引言，然后表扬学生在做到这一点过程中付出的努力，可能比告诉他们自己多么独特，或者说多么有能力改变自己的大脑，更能提升信心。

structure.text: Teaching concrete skills such as how to write an effective introduction to an essay then praising students' effort in getting there
such as how to write an effective introduction to an essay
then praising students' effort in getting there
is probably a far better way of improving confidence
than telling them how unique they are
or indeed how capable they are of changing their own brains

difficult_points: 句首很长的 Teaching ... then praising ... 是动名词短语整体作主语。
such as 后接 how to write ...，作为 concrete skills 的例子。
is probably a far better way 是主句谓语和表语，far 修饰 better 表示程度更强。
than 后面比较的是 telling them ...，其中两个 how 从句作 telling 的直接内容。

expressions.text: an effective introduction to an essay

sentences.sentence: Perhaps growth mindset works best as a philosophy and not an intervention.
sentences.translation: 也许成长型思维作为一种理念时效果最好，而不是作为一种干预手段。

structure.text: as a philosophy and not an intervention

difficult_points: Perhaps 表示作者最终结论带有推测和谨慎语气。
works best as 表示“作为……时效果最好”。
and not an intervention 省略了重复的 as，完整意思是 not as an intervention。
philosophy 与 intervention 形成对比，强调理念层面的价值高于直接干预。
